Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=10-hydroxystrychnine + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = beta-colubrine + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:80935, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:231754, ChEBI:CHEBI:231755; EC=2.1.1.397; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:35794473}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:80936; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:35794473}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=11-demethylbrucine + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = brucine + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:80943, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:231756, ChEBI:CHEBI:231757; EC=2.1.1.397; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:35794473}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:80944; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:35794473};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: O-methyltransferase involved in the biosynthesis of curare monoterpene indole alkaloids (MIAs), natural products such as strychnine, a neurotoxic compound used as a pesticide to control rodents, and its pharmacologically active derivatives, including brucine, used to regulate blood pressure (PubMed:35794473). Curare alkaloids act as animal glycine receptor antagonists (PubMed:35794473). Catalyzes the conversion of 10-OH strychnine to beta-colubrine, and of 11-deMe brucine to brucine (PubMed:35794473). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:35794473}.
